prompt:
Every dashboard exporter we have re-invents 'turn a raw kiosk telemetry record into the card shape the signage backend wants', and half of them blow up under Set-StrictMode when a record doesn't carry the optional fields. Pull it into `shapekit.ps1` in the repo root, dot-sourceable, three functions. Fair warning: the whole test driver runs under `Set-StrictMode -Version Latest`, so probe `$obj.PSObject.Properties` for anything that might be absent — a bare `$record.note` on a note-less record is exactly the crash we're deleting. Build the outputs however you like — Select-Object with calculated properties or an `[ordered]` literal into `[pscustomobject]` — but they must be real PSCustomObjects with a pinned property order, never hashtables.

`ConvertTo-KioskCard -Record <obj>` -> one card object, properties in exactly this order: Kiosk, Site, Uptime, Health, Note.
- Required on the record, checked in this order, first missing one throws `shapekit: record is missing required property 'hostName'` (etc.): hostName, siteCode, uptimeMinutes, errorCount.
- Kiosk/Site are hostName/siteCode renamed, as strings.
- Uptime: uptimeMinutes (an int, though the JSON feed sometimes sends digits as a string — coerce) rendered as `<d>d<h>h`, whole days then whole leftover hours: 3000 -> 2d2h, 90 -> 0d1h, 59 -> 0d0h, 1440 -> 1d0h.
- Health from errorCount: 0 -> ok, 1 through 5 -> watch, 6 and up -> fault.
- Note: the record's note if it has one; a record with no note property OR a null note (the JSON feed does both) -> `(none)`. A present-but-empty note stays the empty string — that's someone who cleared it on purpose.

`Select-CardField -Card <obj> -Name <string[]>` -> a new PSCustomObject carrying only the named properties, in the order GIVEN (not sorted); asking for a property the card doesn't have throws `shapekit: unknown field 'Rack'`.

`Measure-FieldCoverage -Records <object[]> -Name <string>` -> one object, properties Field, Present, Missing: how many records have the named property vs not. Null entries in the array are skipped entirely (the intake feed pads with them).

Acceptance: `pwsh -NoProfile -File test_shapekit.ps1` from the workspace root must pass unmodified — don't touch the test file.
notes: 6P3 item 5: object shaper — calculated/renamed properties, [ordered]->[pscustomobject] construction with pinned property ORDER, and StrictMode-safe property-presence checks via PSObject.Properties. PowerShell-native pins: the driver itself runs Set-StrictMode Latest (strict mode is dynamic, so a naive $record.note inside the dot-sourced library throws PropertyNotFoundStrict — probed 2026-07-16, including that ConvertFrom-Json objects behave the same); property order pinned via PSObject.Properties.Name -join; -is [System.Management.Automation.PSCustomObject] pins real property bags over hashtables; present-with-null vs absent vs present-empty three-way distinction on the note field. Differentiation: ps-pipetally (same chunk) owns pipeline/streaming mechanics — these functions are deliberately argument-shaped single-object shapers; no bash sibling exists because bash has no property bags, the nearest wave-4 analog (bash-fixedw text columns) shapes text not objects. Field renames + computed Uptime/Health are the Select-Object calculated-property surface from the catalog; the prompt explicitly allows either construction idiom.
